playlist_title: February 4 conference: The Age of Reason or the Annihilation of Humanity?

videos.title: How to Avoid Nuclear War: The Nature of Man
videos.description:
Helga Zepp-LaRouche (Germany), Founder, The Schiller Institute: “How to Avoid Nuclear War: The Nature of Man” 

Keynote to the February 4 Schiller Institute conference https://schillerinstitute.com/blog/2023/02/03/conference-the-age-of-reason-or-the-annihilation-of-humanity/

Full transcript available here https://schillerinstitute.com/blog/2023/02/06/keynote-address-how-to-avoid-nuclear-war-the-nature-of-man/

 If the so-called “ordinary citizen” would know how close we are to thermonuclear war, and with that the annihilation of mankind and therefore everything what we lived for and who we love, there would be 8 billion people in the streets demanding an immediate end to the madness of those oligarchical interests, who are driving the human species towards extinction.

The only people who would not be in the streets, would be people who have committed themselves to evil. Boris Johnson and his likes would not be in the streets.

The aim of this conference and many similar activities is to create a world movement of world citizens, who, faced with the biggest crisis in the history of the human species, work together all over the world in order to establish a new paradigm of cooperation instead of confrontation, a new paradigm in international relations, that allows for the life and happiness of every human being on the planet.

We must end the war through negotiations immediately, which is why we are organizing worldwide support for the initiative of Pope Francis to offer the venue of the Vatican for negotiations without preconditions. Fortunately, President Lula of Brazil i

videos.title: Uganda’s Oil Project and Energy Independence
videos.description:
Elison Karuhanga (Uganda), Partner, Kampala Associated Advocates: “Uganda’s Oil Project and Energy Independence”


Address to the February 4 Schiller Institute conference https://schillerinstitute.com/blog/2023/02/03/conference-the-age-of-reason-or-the-annihilation-of-humanity/

Good morning, good afternoon, good evening to everybody joining us.  It is a great honor and privilege to be with the Schiller Institute discussing Uganda's oil project and energy independence.  
 Very briefly, for those who do not know, my name is Elison Karuhanga, I am an advocate [attorney] of the courts of Uganda, a partner in the law firm Kampala Associated Advocates.  I am honored, humbled, and privileged to be sharing my thoughts on an oil project in the middle of Uganda. 
 Uganda is an African country, as you know.  It's a land-locked country that discovered 6.5 billion barrels of oil.  The oil in Uganda is 1.5 billion barrels of it is far recoverable; that's just in 40% of the area.  The country has resolved that it will have a crude oil export pipeline and a refinery, and it will also have commercialized liquefied petroleum gas.  So, this project reached a final investment decision which was made by the investors -- Total Energies and CNOC, the Chinese National Oil Company.  The project is on cost.  The construction of the refinery is going to happen with other investors, but the construction of the East African crude oil pipeline has commenced, and the development of the oil fields that are codenamed Tilenga and Kingfisher.

videos.title: Can Americans Put Aside Their Divisions to Stop Nuclear War?
videos.description:
Diane Sare (United States), candidate for U.S. Senate (New York); Nick Brana (United States), National Chair, People’s Party; Angela McArdle (United States), Chair, Libertarian National Committee: “Can Americans Put Aside Their Divisions to Stop Nuclear War?” 

Address to the February 4 Schiller Institute conference https://schillerinstitute.com/blog/2023/02/03/conference-the-age-of-reason-or-the-annihilation-of-humanity/

Rage Against the War Machine rally, February 19, Washington D.C.
